name: 克劳迪奥·蒙特威尔第
brief: 蒙特威尔第出生于意大利克雷莫纳，是西方音乐史上最关键的转折性人物之一——他亲手将音乐从文艺复兴带入了巴洛克时代。他在曼图亚公爵宫廷服务了二十余年，后来成为威尼斯圣马可大教堂的乐长，在这一欧洲最显赫的音乐职位上任职三十年直至去世。晚年受戒成为神父，但从未停止创作。
briefEn: Born in Cremona, Italy, Monteverdi stands as one of the most pivotal figures in Western music history — the composer who personally ushered music from the Renaissance into the Baroque age. He served at the Mantuan ducal court for over twenty years before becoming maestro di cappella at St. Mark's Basilica in Venice, the most prestigious musical post in Europe, a position he held for thirty years until his death. In his later years he took holy orders, yet never ceased composing.
contribution: 蒙特威尔第是歌剧的奠基者之一。他的《奥菲欧》（1607）被视为第一部真正意义上的歌剧——不再是单纯的牧歌戏剧实验，而是一部完整的音乐戏剧作品。他提出的「第二实践」（Seconda Pratica）理念——音乐应当服从文字，情感表达高于对位法则——从根本上改变了西方音乐的发展方向。
contributionEn: Monteverdi was one of the founders of opera. His L'Orfeo (1607) is widely regarded as the first true opera — no longer a mere experiment in dramatic madrigal but a fully realized work of musical theater. His doctrine of the seconda pratica — that music must serve the text, and emotional expression takes precedence over the rules of counterpoint — fundamentally altered the course of Western music.
style: 蒙特威尔第的风格横跨两个时代。早期作品延续文艺复兴的精致复调，中后期则大胆引入不协和音、戏剧性的声乐技法（如颤音 Trillo、激动风格 Stile Concitato）以及器乐色彩的丰富运用。他的音乐最显著的特点是对人类情感的直接而强烈的表达——愤怒、哀伤、欢乐、渴望，都在他的音乐中获得了前所未有的力度。
styleEn: Monteverdi's style straddles two eras. His early works continue the refined polyphony of the Renaissance, while his middle and late periods boldly introduce dissonance, dramatic vocal techniques such as the trillo and the stile concitato (agitated style), and a richly varied orchestral palette. The most striking hallmark of his music is its direct, visceral expression of human emotion — anger, sorrow, joy, and longing all surge forth with an intensity unprecedented in his time.

famousScores.name: 《奥菲欧》 L'Orfeo
famousScores.desc: 1607年首演，音乐戏剧的开山之作
famousScores.descEn: Premiered in 1607, the founding masterpiece of opera as a genre
famousScores.detail:
1607年2月24日，蒙特威尔第的《奥菲欧》在曼图亚公爵宫廷首演，从此改变了音乐史的走向。此前虽有佩里等人的歌剧实验，但《奥菲欧》是第一部拥有完整戏剧结构、丰富管弦乐编制和深刻情感表达的歌剧。蒙特威尔第为这部作品动用了约四十件乐器，包括管风琴、竖琴、小提琴、短号和长号等，在当时堪称前所未有的阵容。

奥菲欧下冥界救妻的故事给了蒙特威尔第充分施展戏剧才华的空间。第三幕中奥菲欧用歌声打动冥王的段落「强大的精灵」（Possente spirto），是声乐炫技与情感深度的完美结合。这部歌剧不仅确立了歌剧作为独立艺术形式的地位，更为此后四百年的歌剧创作树立了标杆——音乐必须服务于戏剧，情感表达高于一切技术规则。
famousScores.detailEn:
On February 24, 1607, Monteverdi's L'Orfeo premiered at the ducal court in Mantua and changed the trajectory of music history. Though earlier operatic experiments existed, L'Orfeo was the first to possess a fully realized dramatic structure, a rich orchestral palette of some forty instruments — organ, harp, violins, cornetts, trombones — and a depth of emotional expression that no previous work had achieved.

The myth of Orpheus descending to the underworld to rescue his wife gave Monteverdi the perfect vehicle for his dramatic gifts. In Act III, Orpheus's aria 'Possente spirto,' in which he sings to move the king of the underworld, remains one of the most extraordinary fusions of vocal virtuosity and emotional depth in all of opera. L'Orfeo established opera as an independent art form and set a standard that would guide four centuries of operatic creation: music must serve drama, and emotional truth must prevail over technical rules.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, English Baroque Soloists, John Eliot Gardiner
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Greatest Baroque Classics - Compilation by Various Artists

famousScores.name: 《波佩亚的加冕》 L'incoronazione di Poppea
famousScores.desc: 晚年歌剧杰作，以历史题材展现人性的复杂
famousScores.descEn: A late operatic masterwork that plumbs the complexities of human nature through historical drama
famousScores.detail:
《波佩亚的加冕》创作于1643年，是蒙特威尔第生命最后一年的作品，也是他留给后世的终极歌剧杰作。这是歌剧史上第一部取材于真实历史事件而非神话的歌剧——讲述罗马皇帝尼禄抛弃妻子奥塔维娅、迎娶情妇波佩亚的故事。

这部歌剧最令人震撼之处在于其道德上的复杂性：通过不忠和野心获胜的恋人最终得到了加冕的荣耀，而忠贞的妻子和正直的哲学家塞涅卡却遭到放逐和死亡。结尾处尼禄与波佩亚的二重唱「我凝视你」（Pur ti miro）是音乐史上最动人的爱情段落之一，其旋律之优美几乎让听众忘记了这份爱情背后的道德代价。蒙特威尔第在七十六岁时依然展现出对人性矛盾的深邃理解，这部作品至今仍是世界各大歌剧院的常演剧目。
famousScores.detailEn:
Composed in 1643, the final year of Monteverdi's life, L'incoronazione di Poppea stands as his ultimate operatic masterpiece. It was the first opera in history to draw on real historical events rather than mythology — telling the story of Emperor Nero abandoning his wife Ottavia to crown his mistress Poppea as empress.

What makes this opera so striking is its moral complexity: the lovers who triumph through infidelity and ambition are rewarded with imperial glory, while the faithful wife and the upright philosopher Seneca meet exile and death. The closing duet 'Pur ti miro,' in which Nero and Poppea celebrate their love, is among the most ravishing passages in all of music — its beauty is so overwhelming that listeners nearly forget the moral cost behind it. At seventy-six, Monteverdi demonstrated an astonishing understanding of human contradiction. The work remains a staple of opera houses worldwide.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, Danielle de Niese, Andreas Scholl, The English Concert, Harry Bicket
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: L'incoronazione di Poppea / Act 3: Pur ti miro

famousScores.name: 《圣母晚祷》 Vespro della Beata Vergine
famousScores.desc: 1610年的宏大宗教作品，融合新旧两种风格
famousScores.descEn: A monumental sacred work of 1610 that fuses the old and new styles into a single grand design
famousScores.detail:
1610年出版的《圣母晚祷》是蒙特威尔第最宏伟的宗教作品，也是整个巴洛克早期最重要的宗教音乐之一。这部作品的创作动机至今成谜——有学者认为它是蒙特威尔第为求取威尼斯圣马可大教堂乐长一职而精心准备的「求职信」，也有人认为它是为曼图亚宫廷的特定庆典而作。

作品的规模令人叹为观止：包含了传统的格里高利圣咏、文艺复兴风格的严格对位、以及最新锐的巴洛克协奏风格，宛如一部音乐风格的百科全书。其中的「圣母颂」（Ave Maris Stella）庄严肃穆，而「两位炽天使」（Duo Seraphim）中三位男高音的华丽对唱则展现了全新的巴洛克声乐美学。蒙特威尔第在一部作品中完成了从旧世界到新世界的过渡，这种大胆的综合手法在音乐史上绝无仅有。
famousScores.detailEn:
Published in 1610, the Vespers of the Blessed Virgin is Monteverdi's most monumental sacred composition and one of the most important works of sacred music from the early Baroque. The motivation behind its creation remains a mystery — some scholars believe it was Monteverdi's elaborate 'job application' for the position of maestro di cappella at St. Mark's in Venice, while others argue it was written for a specific celebration at the Mantuan court.

The scale of the work is breathtaking: it encompasses traditional Gregorian chant, strict Renaissance counterpoint, and the newest Baroque concertato style, reading like an encyclopedia of musical approaches. The 'Ave Maris Stella' is solemnly majestic, while the 'Duo Seraphim' — a dazzling interplay among three tenors — showcases a wholly new Baroque vocal aesthetic. In a single work, Monteverdi accomplished the transition from the old musical world to the new, a feat of synthesis unmatched in music history.
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Monteverdi: Vespro della Beata Vergine, 1610, etc. - Compilation by Claudio Monteverdi
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Vespro della Beata Vergine - Performing Edition by John Eliot Gardiner: 1. Deus in adjutorium meum intende

famousScores.name: 《坦克雷迪与克洛琳达的战斗》
famousScores.nameEn: Il combattimento di Tancredi e Clorinda
famousScores.desc: 首创「激动风格」（Stile Concitato），用弦乐震音描绘战斗
famousScores.descEn: The first work to employ the stile concitato, using string tremolo to depict the fury of battle
famousScores.detail:
《坦克雷迪与克洛琳达的战斗》取材于塔索的史诗《解放的耶路撒冷》，讲述十字军骑士坦克雷迪在夜战中误杀了自己深爱的萨拉森女战士克洛琳达的悲剧。蒙特威尔第于1624年在威尼斯首演了这部作品，后来收入第八卷牧歌集。

这部作品最具革命性的贡献是「激动风格」（Stile Concitato）的发明——蒙特威尔第要求弦乐演奏快速的重复音符（震音）来模拟战斗的激烈与紧张，这在音乐史上是第一次。这种技法后来成为巴洛克乃至所有管弦乐写作的标准手段。作品由一位叙述者、男高音（坦克雷迪）和女高音（克洛琳达）演唱，配合弦乐与通奏低音，以半戏剧化的形式呈现。从激烈的搏斗到克洛琳达临终的宁静洗礼，情感的巨大跨度被蒙特威尔第处理得惊心动魄。
famousScores.detailEn:
Il combattimento di Tancredi e Clorinda draws on Tasso's epic Jerusalem Delivered, telling the tragedy of the Crusader knight Tancredi who, in a nighttime battle, unwittingly kills the Saracen warrior woman he loves, Clorinda. Monteverdi premiered the work in Venice in 1624, later including it in his Eighth Book of Madrigals.

The work's most revolutionary contribution is the invention of the stile concitato — the 'agitated style' — in which Monteverdi instructed string players to perform rapid repeated notes (tremolo) to simulate the intensity of combat. This was the first time such a technique had been used in music, and it went on to become a standard device in Baroque and indeed all orchestral writing. Scored for a narrator, tenor (Tancredi), and soprano (Clorinda) with strings and continuo, the piece unfolds in semi-dramatic fashion. The emotional arc — from the ferocity of battle to the serene tranquility of Clorinda's deathbed baptism — is handled with shattering power.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, Catherine Bott, Andrew King, John Mark Ainsley, New London Consort, Philip Pickett
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Il Combattimento di Tancredi e Clorinda, SV 153: 1. Tancredi, che Clorinda un homo stima

famousScores.name: 《牧歌集》第八卷「战争与爱情牧歌集」
famousScores.desc: 晚年牧歌巨作，总结了一生的创作探索
famousScores.descEn: A monumental late collection of madrigals summing up a lifetime of creative exploration
famousScores.detail:
1638年出版的第八卷牧歌集《战争与爱情牧歌集》是蒙特威尔第毕生牧歌创作的集大成之作。全集分为「战争牧歌」和「爱情牧歌」两大部分，汇聚了他从1620年代到1630年代创作的精华。这不仅仅是一本歌曲集，更是一部关于人类激情的百科全书。

蒙特威尔第在序言中系统阐述了他的情感理论：古希腊音乐能表达平静、柔和和激动三种情感，而当代音乐只掌握了前两种。他通过「激动风格」补全了这一缺失。集中既包含宏大的戏剧性场景——如《坦克雷迪与克洛琳达的战斗》和《仙女的哀歌》，也有精致的室内牧歌和舞蹈歌曲。从规模、野心和艺术成就来看，这部牧歌集标志着文艺复兴牧歌体裁的最终辉煌与告别。
famousScores.detailEn:
Published in 1638, the Eighth Book of Madrigals — subtitled 'Madrigals of War and Love' — represents the crowning achievement of Monteverdi's lifelong engagement with the madrigal. Divided into 'warlike' and 'amorous' halves, it gathers the finest works he composed from the 1620s through the 1630s. It is not merely a collection of songs but an encyclopedia of human passion.

In the preface, Monteverdi systematically laid out his theory of emotions: ancient Greek music could express calm, soft, and agitated affects, but contemporary music had mastered only the first two. Through the stile concitato he filled this gap. The collection includes grand dramatic scenes — the Combattimento and the Lamento della Ninfa among them — alongside refined chamber madrigals and dance songs. In scope, ambition, and artistic achievement, this volume marks both the ultimate glory and the farewell of the Renaissance madrigal tradition.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, Concerto Italiano, Rinaldo Alessandrini
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Claudio Monteverdi: Lamento Della Ninfa (Madrigali del Ottavio Libro)
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Hor Che'l Ciel e la Terra E'l Vento Tace

famousScores.name: 《阿里安娜的悲歌》 Lamento d'Arianna
famousScores.desc: 歌剧《阿里安娜》中唯一存世的片段，17世纪最著名的声乐曲
famousScores.descEn: The sole surviving fragment of the opera L'Arianna, and the most famous vocal piece of the 17th century
famousScores.detail:
1608年，蒙特威尔第为曼图亚宫廷创作了歌剧《阿里安娜》。整部歌剧的乐谱已经散佚，唯独这首「悲歌」因其巨大的影响力而以多种形式流传至今。据记载，首演时这首悲歌令在场所有贵妇潸然泪下。阿里安娜被忒修斯抛弃在荒岛上，从悲痛到愤怒再到绝望，每一种情感都在音乐中得到了淋漓尽致的表达。

蒙特威尔第后来将这首独唱悲歌改编为五声部牧歌版本，收入第六卷牧歌集（1614年）；又创作了一个宗教改编版「圣母的悲歌」。这首作品在整个17世纪被无数作曲家模仿，催生了「悲歌」（Lamento）这一重要的声乐体裁——以下行半音阶低音线为标志，表达哀伤与失落。可以说，蒙特威尔第用一首歌创造了一个音乐传统。
famousScores.detailEn:
In 1608, Monteverdi composed the opera L'Arianna for the Mantuan court. The full score has been lost; only this lament survived, preserved in multiple versions thanks to its extraordinary impact. Contemporary accounts report that at the premiere, every noblewoman in the audience was moved to tears. Arianna, abandoned on a desolate island by Theseus, passes through grief, rage, and despair — each emotion rendered with devastating vividness in the music.

Monteverdi later reworked the solo lament as a five-voice madrigal for his Sixth Book (1614) and also created a sacred contrafactum, the 'Pianto della Madonna.' The piece was imitated by countless composers throughout the seventeenth century and gave birth to the lamento as a major vocal genre — characterized by a descending chromatic bass line expressing sorrow and loss. With a single song, Monteverdi created an entire musical tradition.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, Concerto Italiano, Rinaldo Alessandrini
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Monteverdi: Il Sesto Libro de Madrigali

famousScores.name: 《仙女的悲歌》 Lamento della Ninfa
famousScores.desc: 固定低音之上的即兴式独唱，巴洛克声乐的巅峰之一
famousScores.descEn: An improvisatory solo over an ostinato bass, among the pinnacles of Baroque vocal art
famousScores.detail:
《仙女的悲歌》收录于第八卷牧歌集，是蒙特威尔第最令人心碎的作品之一。作品描绘一位被爱人抛弃的仙女在晨光中哭泣，三位男声牧羊人在旁旁白评述。仙女的独唱建立在一个不断重复的四音下行低音线（A-G#-F#-E）之上——这个固定低音循环了整整十七次，形成一种催眠般的悲伤。

蒙特威尔第在乐谱上写下了一条革命性的指示：仙女的歌唱应该「随心灵的情感而行」（al tempo dell'affetto del animo），而非严格跟随节拍。这意味着女高音可以在固定低音的框架上自由地拉伸和压缩节奏，用即兴式的表达来传递痛苦。这种自由与约束的张力——无尽循环的低音如同命运的枷锁，而仙女的歌声则如同试图挣脱束缚的灵魂——创造出令人难以忘怀的艺术效果。
famousScores.detailEn:
Included in the Eighth Book of Madrigals, the Lamento della Ninfa is one of Monteverdi's most heartbreaking creations. It depicts a nymph weeping at dawn after her lover has abandoned her, while three male-voice shepherds narrate and comment. The nymph's solo is built over a relentlessly repeating four-note descending bass (A-G sharp-F sharp-E) that cycles seventeen times, creating an almost hypnotic aura of grief.

Monteverdi wrote a revolutionary instruction in the score: the nymph should sing 'al tempo dell'affetto del animo' — according to the emotion of her soul — rather than in strict time. This means the soprano is free to stretch and compress her rhythm over the ostinato framework, using improvisatory expression to convey anguish. The tension between freedom and constraint — the endlessly cycling bass like the shackles of fate, the nymph's voice straining to break free — produces an unforgettable artistic effect.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, Concerto Italiano, Rinaldo Alessandrini
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: The Golden Age of Italian Baroque Music - Compilation by Various Artists

famousScores.name: 《尤利西斯还乡记》 Il ritorno d'Ulisse in patria
famousScores.desc: 1640年首演的歌剧，将荷马史诗搬上音乐舞台
famousScores.descEn: An opera premiered in 1640, bringing Homer's epic to the musical stage
famousScores.detail:
《尤利西斯还乡记》于1640年在威尼斯首演，是蒙特威尔第晚年重返歌剧创作的第一部作品。改编自荷马《奥德赛》的后半段，讲述尤利西斯（奥德修斯）在特洛伊战争后历经磨难返回家园伊塔卡，与忠贞的妻子佩内洛佩重逢的故事。这是威尼斯歌剧史上第一部脱离神话牧歌框架、具有真正戏剧深度的作品。

蒙特威尔第在这部歌剧中展现了惊人的人物刻画能力：佩内洛佩的坚贞与孤独、尤利西斯的智慧与疲惫、求婚者们的傲慢与愚蠢，都通过各不相同的音乐语言得到了生动的呈现。佩内洛佩在第一幕中的独白是全剧最感人的段落——二十年的等待与思念凝聚在一段哀婉的宣叙调中。这部歌剧的重新发现与复演是20世纪古乐复兴运动的重要成果之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Il ritorno d'Ulisse in patria premiered in Venice in 1640 and marked Monteverdi's return to opera in his final years. Adapted from the second half of Homer's Odyssey, it tells the story of Ulysses returning to Ithaca after the Trojan War to reunite with his faithful wife Penelope. It was the first Venetian opera to move beyond pastoral mythology toward genuine dramatic depth.

Monteverdi's character portrayal is astonishing: Penelope's steadfastness and loneliness, Ulysses' wisdom and weariness, the suitors' arrogance and foolishness — each is vividly rendered through distinct musical language. Penelope's opening monologue in Act I is the opera's most moving passage, distilling twenty years of waiting and longing into a single, plaintive recitative. The rediscovery and revival of this opera has been one of the great achievements of the early music movement in the twentieth century.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, René Jacobs, Concerto Vocale
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Monteverdi: Il ritorno d'Ulisse in patria
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Il ritorno d'Ulisse in patria: Sinfonia

famousScores.name: 《牧歌集》第五卷
famousScores.nameEn: Madrigals, Book 5 (Il quinto libro de madrigali)
famousScores.desc: 「第二实践」的宣言之作，引发了著名的阿图西论战
famousScores.descEn: The manifesto of the seconda pratica, which sparked the famous Artusi controversy
famousScores.detail:
1605年出版的第五卷牧歌集是音乐史上一个转折点。正是这些作品中大胆的不协和音处理，引发了保守派理论家阿图西的猛烈攻击——他在1600年的文章中谴责蒙特威尔第「违反了对位法的一切规则」。蒙特威尔第则在这本牧歌集的序言中首次提出了「第二实践」的概念作为回击：音乐存在两种实践，旧的实践以和声规则为主宰，新的实践以文字和情感为主宰。

这卷牧歌集的另一个重要创新是引入了通奏低音（Basso Continuo）——最后六首牧歌都标注了数字低音声部，这标志着文艺复兴纯声乐牧歌向巴洛克器乐伴奏声乐的过渡。集中的「残酷的阿玛丽利」（Cruda Amarilli）正是阿图西重点批评的对象，其中的不协和音处理在今天听来自然流畅，但在当时却是惊世骇俗的革命。
famousScores.detailEn:
Published in 1605, the Fifth Book of Madrigals marks a turning point in music history. It was the bold dissonance treatment in these works that provoked the furious attack by the conservative theorist Giovanni Maria Artusi, who in 1600 denounced Monteverdi for 'violating every rule of counterpoint.' In the preface to this very book, Monteverdi fired back by articulating for the first time his concept of the seconda pratica: there exist two practices in music — the old, where harmony rules over text, and the new, where text and emotion rule over harmony.

Another crucial innovation in this volume is the introduction of basso continuo — the last six madrigals include a figured bass part, marking the transition from the purely vocal Renaissance madrigal to the instrumentally accompanied vocal music of the Baroque. The madrigal 'Cruda Amarilli,' the principal target of Artusi's criticism, contains dissonance treatment that sounds perfectly natural today but was revolutionary in its own time.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, Krijn Koetsveld, Il Nuove Musiche
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Madrigals, Book 5, SV 94–106: Cruda amarilli, SV 94

famousScores.name: 《道德与精神之林》 Selva morale e spirituale
famousScores.desc: 1640年出版的大型宗教作品集，威尼斯时期的精华汇编
famousScores.descEn: A major sacred collection published in 1640, compiling the finest works of his Venetian years
famousScores.detail:
《道德与精神之林》出版于1640年，是蒙特威尔第在威尼斯担任圣马可大教堂乐长期间创作的宗教作品精华集。「Selva」意为「森林」，暗喻其中作品种类之丰富——包括弥撒曲、赞美诗、经文歌、圣母颂歌等多种体裁，共计四十余首作品，规模庞大。

与《圣母晚祷》的宏大统一不同，这部作品集的魅力在于其多样性。从庄严的八声部弥撒到亲密的独唱经文歌，从严格的古风对位到华丽的协奏风格，蒙特威尔第展现了他作为威尼斯最重要音乐家的全部功力。其中一些作品是世俗牧歌的宗教改编版，比如著名的「阿里安娜的悲歌」被改写为「圣母的眼泪」。这部作品集是了解蒙特威尔第晚年宗教音乐创作的最重要文献。
famousScores.detailEn:
Published in 1640, the Selva morale e spirituale is a grand anthology of sacred works composed during Monteverdi's tenure as maestro di cappella at St. Mark's Basilica in Venice. 'Selva' means 'forest,' alluding to the rich variety within — masses, hymns, motets, and Marian canticles totaling over forty pieces.

Unlike the monumental unity of the Vespers, the charm of this collection lies in its diversity. From a majestic eight-voice mass to intimate solo motets, from strict stile antico counterpoint to brilliant concertato writing, Monteverdi displays the full range of his powers as Venice's foremost musician. Several pieces are sacred reworkings of secular madrigals — notably the famous Lamento d'Arianna, recast as the 'Pianto della Madonna.' This collection is the single most important document for understanding Monteverdi's late sacred output.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Claudio Monteverdi, The Sixteen, Harry Christophers
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: The Selva morale e spirituale Collection

influence: 蒙特威尔第开创的歌剧传统是整个巴洛克音乐最重要的遗产之一，威尼斯歌剧学派直接从他发源。他的「第二实践」理念为巴洛克情感美学奠定了理论基础。他对不协和音的大胆使用、对乐器色彩的探索、对戏剧与音乐关系的思考，深刻影响了亨德尔、格鲁克乃至瓦格纳。他是古代音乐与现代音乐之间最重要的桥梁。
influenceEn: The operatic tradition Monteverdi inaugurated is one of the most important legacies of the entire Baroque era; the Venetian opera school descended directly from him. His seconda pratica doctrine laid the theoretical foundation for Baroque expressive aesthetics. His bold use of dissonance, his exploration of instrumental color, and his profound thinking about the relationship between drama and music deeply influenced Handel, Gluck, and ultimately Wagner. He remains the most vital bridge between ancient and modern music.
